desc: Running the module unconditionally replaces the configured users file with hard-coded sample data, destroying existing data.
getTopUser can return a randomly selected user when allow_random is true, violating its top-user behavior.
loadAndProcessUsers defaults flag to true and consequently treats every loaded user as active regardless of stored data.
The bare exception handler converts any JSON parsing failure into an empty dataset, silently hiding corrupt input.
The bare except also catches exceptions such as KeyboardInterrupt and SystemExit that should not be treated as invalid JSON.
The loader assumes the JSON root is iterable and every element is a mapping, so valid JSON with another shape can crash at item.get.
User fields are not type-validated, allowing comparisons, averaging, or string concatenation to fail on otherwise valid JSON values of unexpected types.
getTopUser returns either None, a User, or a dictionary based on the winning score, forcing callers to depend on unrelated type-switching behavior.
The global cache retains old results on early returns and exposes the same mutable list returned to callers, making cached state stale or externally mutable.
The relative data path is resolved against the process working directory rather than the module location, so the program can read or overwrite an unintended users.json.
